name: South Carolina Governor's School for Science and Mathematics
about: The South Carolina Governor's School for Science and Mathematics (GSSM) is a public, tuition-free residential high school in Hartsville for academically talented South Carolina juniors and seniors. Its two-year in-residence program offers an intensive STEM-focused curriculum with more than 50 advanced courses, many exceeding the AP level, and mentored research. All residential students live on campus and the program is open to South Carolina residents at no tuition cost.
college_matriculation: 100% of residential graduates attend college; graduates routinely matriculate to selective universities including Clemson, the University of South Carolina, Duke, Georgia Tech and other national institutions.
financial_aid_notes: Two-year in-residence program is tuition-free for South Carolina residents. Students pay an annual meal fee (about $3,360 in 2025-26); financial aid is available to cover fees and meal plans, and no accepted student is denied for inability to pay.

name: Baylor School
about: Baylor School is an independent, coeducational college-preparatory day and boarding school in Chattanooga, Tennessee, founded in 1893. It enrolls roughly 1,120 students in grades 6-12 on a 690-acre campus along the Tennessee River, with boarding offered in grades 9-12. Once a military school, Baylor is known for its strong academics and one of the most successful athletics programs in the state.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend selective universities including Cornell, Duke, Princeton, Stanford, Harvard and Georgetown.
accreditation: SAIS (Southern Association of Independent Schools); member NAIS, TAIS
notable_alumni: Brian Gottfried (tennis), Hugh Beaumont (actor), Fob James (Governor of Alabama)
financial_aid_notes: About 40% of students receive financial aid; need-based aid is awarded and the application for aid does not affect admission decisions.

name: McCallie School
about: McCallie School is an independent, all-boys college-preparatory day and boarding school on Missionary Ridge in Chattanooga, Tennessee, founded in 1905. It enrolls about 992 students in grades 6-12 on a 140-acre campus, with boarding offered in the upper school. The school is non-denominationally Christian and is the brother school to the Girls Preparatory School.
college_matriculation: Graduates matriculate to a wide range of selective colleges and universities across the U.S.
notable_alumni: Ted Turner (CNN founder), Howard Baker Jr. (U.S. Senator), Jon Meacham (author/editor), Pat Robertson, Zach Wamp (U.S. Representative)
financial_aid_notes: About 42% of students receive financial aid.

name: St. Andrew's-Sewanee School
about: St. Andrew's-Sewanee School is an independent, coeducational Episcopal college-preparatory day and boarding school in Sewanee, Tennessee, serving grades 6-12. Formed in 1981 from the merger of St. Andrew's School (founded 1905) and Sewanee Academy, it traces its roots to 1868 and sits on a 550-acre campus atop the Cumberland Plateau. The school emphasizes small classes, a close-knit community, and an Episcopal identity that welcomes students of all faiths.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend a broad range of colleges and universities, including the nearby University of the South (Sewanee).
notable_alumni: James Agee (author), Saxby Chambliss (U.S. Senator), Elisabeth Rohm (actress), Kix Brooks (musician), Stuart Margolin (actor)
financial_aid_notes: The school awards roughly $2.1 million in financial aid annually; in 2024-25, 47% of students received a financial aid grant. Applying for aid does not affect admission decisions.

name: The Webb School
about: The Webb School is an independent, coeducational college-preparatory day and boarding school in Bell Buckle, Tennessee, founded in 1870 by William R. 'Sawney' Webb. It serves grades 6-12 on a 145-acre rural campus and describes itself as the oldest continuously operating boarding school in the South. The school offers seven-day boarding for upper-school students drawn from many states and countries.
college_matriculation: 100% college acceptance; graduates attend a wide range of colleges and universities.
accreditation: SAIS (Southern Association of Independent Schools); member TABS, NAIS, TAIS
notable_alumni: The Webb School counts numerous governors, U.S. senators, Rhodes Scholars and military officers among its graduates.
financial_aid_notes: Approximately $3.5 million in financial aid and scholarships is awarded annually; need is assessed via the Clarity Application and does not affect admission. Legacy scholarships are also offered.

name: Brook Hill School
about: The Brook Hill School is an independent, Christ-centered college-preparatory day and boarding school in Bullard, Texas, just south of Tyler, founded in 1997. It serves students in grades PreK-12 in its day school and grades 8-12 in its boarding program on a 280-acre campus that also houses the American Freedom Museum. The school enrolls students from East Texas and around the world, with about 30 countries represented.
college_matriculation: 100% college acceptance; up to 100 college credit hours available to students through AP and dual-credit courses.
accreditation: Cognia (SACS/Southern Association of Colleges and Schools)
financial_aid_notes: Financial aid is available; the school partners with FACTS (domestic) and FAST (international) to assess need. International boarding students pay an additional fee.
